decisions.title: Why Plan Before Execute
decisions.description: A TodoWrite tool forces the agent to articulate its plan as a structured list of tasks before executing any of them. This is not just good practice — it fundamentally changes agent behavior. Without a plan, agents tend to act greedily, solving whatever is immediately in front of them. With a written plan, the agent can reason about task ordering, dependencies, and completeness.
decisions.alternatives: We considered letting the agent plan implicitly through chain-of-thought reasoning. However, an explicit todo list is inspectable by the user, modifiable at runtime, and serves as a progress tracker. Implicit plans disappear once the context scrolls away.

zh.title: 为什么先规划再执行
zh.description: TodoWrite 工具强制 Agent 在执行任何操作之前，先将计划表达为结构化的任务列表。这不仅是好习惯，更从根本上改变了 Agent 的行为模式。没有计划时，Agent 倾向于贪心地解决眼前的问题；有了书面计划后，Agent 能够推理任务的顺序、依赖关系和完整性。

decisions.title: Why Nag Reminders Help Completion
decisions.description: After each tool execution, the system injects a reminder of incomplete todo items into the conversation. This 'nagging' mechanism counteracts a common failure mode: the agent gets distracted by a subtask and forgets the original plan. By constantly re-surfacing the todo list, the agent stays on track and completes all planned steps.
decisions.alternatives: We tried relying on the agent's memory alone, but LLMs frequently lose track of multi-step plans, especially as context grows. A periodic summary of remaining tasks was considered, but per-turn reminders proved more reliable at preventing drift.

zh.title: 为什么反复提醒有助于任务完成
zh.description: 每次工具执行后，系统会将未完成的待办事项注入对话中。这种「唠叨」机制针对的是一种常见的失败模式：Agent 被子任务分散注意力，忘记了原始计划。通过不断地重新呈现待办列表，Agent 能保持在正轨上，完成所有计划步骤。
